title: indigofera linnaei
intro: Indigofera linnaei, also known as Indian snuff or fever plant, is a plant species native to India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the Fabaceae family and used for its medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of the Indigofera linnaei plant contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Indigofera linnaei can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or tincture, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
